commit_scope: Add a distinct Windows ARM64 Hati-OS target floor on the shared ARM64 assembler lane while preserving Windows x86_64 as the separate Intel/AMD x64 instruction lane.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/hati-os-targets.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-lower.fk form-stdlib/jit-tensor-emit.fk form-stdlib/tests/jit-metal-lanes-band.fk
cd form && bash scripts/fourth-arm-gate.sh hati-os-targets jit-metal-lanes
subagent Sagan: reran git diff --check, verify_windows_fkwu_self_jit.ps1, both focused validate commands, and fourth-arm-gate with no edits
local_validation.notes: Focused validation reports hati-os-targets verdict 2097151 and jit-metal-lanes verdict 8191 with fourth arm attached and 0 divergent samples. The fourth-arm gate reports PASS-4WAY for both bands. The Windows self-JIT verifier remains the x64 lane proof and reports cold=317811 hot=317811 njit=26, runtime PATH without Go/clang/LLVM, and FLOOR target=windows-x86_64 alias=windows-amd64 instruction_lane=x64. Subagent Sagan independently verified the same commands and made no file changes.

ci_validation.notes: Not pushed yet; PR CI has not run for this branch.

commands: Hostinger Auto Deploy on main after merge
deploy_validation.notes: This slice changes Form target catalog, proof naming, docs, and evidence only. Public route behavior is unchanged before merge.

phase_gate.gate: Windows ARM64 must be first-class and distinct from Windows x86_64: ARM64 shares the macOS/Linux/Android assembler lane but has a Windows PE/COFF linker-image surface, while x86_64 remains the Intel/AMD x64 instruction lane.
phase_gate.next: Rebase, run local PR gates, push branch, open PR, monitor CI, then merge only if checks pass.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The catalog now exposes windows-arm64 as an ARM64 Windows target with a PE/COFF image surface and Form-native ARM64 assembler runtime, while windows-x86_64 remains the x64 Intel/AMD lane with the current fkwu self-JIT verifier.
public_endpoints: local-only: Form/fkwu runtime and target catalog; no public HTTP endpoint changed
test_flows: Run focused Form validation over hati-os-targets and assert verdict 2097151 with fourth arm attached.
Run focused Form validation over jit-metal-lanes and assert verdict 8191 with fourth arm attached.
Run the hati-os-targets and jit-metal-lanes fourth-arm gate and assert PASS-4WAY for both.
Run the Windows fkwu self-JIT verifier and assert cold=317811, hot=317811, njit>0, and instruction_lane=x64 for windows-x86_64.
Run independent sub-agent validation over the same checks and confirm no edits were made.
